message:
[medium] plaid: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

normalize_base_url at connector.rs:1173 validated scheme (https/http),
host presence, and IP-literal restriction, but returned
`trimmed.trim_end_matches('/').to_string()` — so query, fragment, and
userinfo components in the raw input survived into PlaidConfig.base_url.
PlaidClient then concatenates via format!("{}/link/token/create",
self.base_url) and similar in every request method (client.rs:99 /
117 / 133 / 157 / 183 / …). A configure payload like

    {"client_id":"c","secret":"s","environment":"sandbox","base_url":"https://sandbox.plaid.com?leak=x"}

would pass normalize_base_url and leak `?leak=x` on every Plaid API
request, with the endpoint path parsed as part of the query. Userinfo
(`https://attacker:pw@sandbox.plaid.com`) would bake into every
request URL and, while Plaid auth is client_id/secret in the JSON
body rather than an Authorization header, the userinfo on the URL
still leaks credentials to any proxy or log that captures the URL.

- normalize_base_url now rejects userinfo (username or password) and
  query/fragment components between the scheme check and the host /
  IP-literal checks. Reuses the existing reqwest::Url parse.
- Five new unit tests: accept clean sandbox.plaid.com, reject query /
  fragment / userinfo, and a path-based substring regression
  documenting that normalize_base_url intentionally does NOT enforce
  the PLAID_HOSTS allowlist (that is handled downstream at doctor
  time via is_plaid_host).

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo test -p
fcp-plaid --lib normalize_base_url → 5 passed; 0 failed. Pattern
matches the helpers shipped in hubspot / clickup / monday / bitbucket
/ intercom / dropbox / mailchimp / algolia this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
